editorial.title: Economic rally should support youth employment
editorial.body: Despite the rally of semiconductor-led exports and the nation's bourses, the country's employment rate declined in May for the first time in 17 mon...
editorial.pullQuote: Despite the rally of semiconductor-led exports and the nation's bourses, the country's employment rate declined in May.
summary: Semiconductor exports and stock market gains mask a troubling employment decline in May.
Job creation stalled for the first time in 17 months despite economic strength elsewhere.
Policymakers must target youth employment to capitalize on the current economic rally.
editorial.stance: Economic gains must translate into real job opportunities, especially for young workers.
summaryTr: 반도체 수출 호조와 주식 시장 상승이 5월의 우려스러운 고용 감소를 가리고 있다.
경제가 다른 분야에서 강세를 보이고 있음에도 불구하고 17개월 만에 처음으로 일자리 창출이 정체됐다.
정책 입안자들은 현재의 경제 호황을 활용하기 위해 청년 고용을 목표로 삼아야 한다.
editorial.bodyTr: 반도체 중심의 수출 랠리와 국내 증시의 상승에도 불구하고, 국내 고용률은 5월 17개월 만에 처음으로 하락했다.

leanScore.label: Lean progressive
leanScore.rationale: Criticizes economic indicators (exports, stock market) as masking structural employment failure and demands targeted intervention for youth workers, framing job creation as a policy priority rather than market outcome.

editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Court Halts Government's Attempt to Silence Daejangdong Scandal with Reassignment
summaryTr: The Seoul Administrative Court issued a ruling to cancel the demotion decision in the Daejang-dong personnel reshuffle.
Prosecutor General Jung Yu-mi was demoted after refusing to drop her appeal.
The court has put the brakes on the administration's personnel retaliation.
editorials.bodyTr: The court has put the brakes on the administration's reassignment of prosecutor Jung Yu-mi. The Seoul Administrative Court's Administrative Division 5 ruled that the Justice Ministry must rescind its personnel action against Jung Yu-mi, a senior prosecutor who was transferred after opposing the prosecution's decision to abandon an appeal in the Daejang-dong case. editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Insulting Police at Protests: Defending Voting Rights Requires Respecting Authority Too
summaryTr: Protests over ballot shortages in the June 3 local elections continue to diminish in scale.
The shrinking size of demonstrations does not reduce public anger over violations of voting rights.
Respect for public authority is necessary for protecting voting rights to gain legitimacy.
editorials.bodyTr:
Insulting Police at Protest Sites: If We're to Protect Voting Rights, We Must Respect Public Authority

The scale of protests at Seoul's Olympic Park against the ballot shortage issue in the June 3 local elections is reportedly shrinking. However, the decline in demonstrations does not diminish the public's widespread anger over the infringement of voting rights or the seriousness of the problem.

editorial.title: [사설] 비밀 유출 논란 자초한 국방장관 발언
editorial.body: 입력 종합 26면 안규백 국방부 장관이 지난달 31일(현지시간) 제23차 아시아 안보회의(샹그릴라 대화)가 열리고 있는 싱가포르 샹그릴라 호텔에서 취재진과 만나 현안에 대해 설명하고 있다. [연합뉴스] 전시작전통제권(전작권) 전환을 위한 한·미 양국의 평가 수준을 공개한 안규백 국방부 장관의 발언이 비밀 유출 논란에 휩싸였다. 안 장관은 지난달 31일 싱가포르 출장 중 “한·미 양국은 2020년에 전작권 전환 조건의 94%가 이미 충족됐다고 합의한 것을 비롯해 우리의 능력에 대해 충분히 설명했다”고 밝혔다. 이날 아시아안보회의(샹그릴라 대화)에 참석한 미국 의원들을 만나 한국군의 전작권 행사 능력을 설명하고 협조를 요청했다는 것이다. 한·미는 조건이 갖춰지면 전작권을 한국군에 이양하기로 하고, 구체적인 매뉴얼을 만들어 평가를 진행 중이다. 하지만 구체적인 평가 항목이나 이행 수준은 한·미 연합의 비밀로 간주하고 있다. 자칫 한·미 연합 전력 수준과 방어 태세가 북한 등에 공개될 것을 우려해서다. 그런데 국방부 수장이 나서 양국 군 당국이 공개를 꺼려 온 94%라는 구체적인 수치를 언급한 것이다. 논란이 일자 국방부는 “조속한 전작권 회복과 관련한 국민의 알권리 보장과 이해를 돕기 위해 개략적인 수치를 제시한 것”이라며 “구체적인 내용을 적시한 게 아니어서 한·미 연합 비밀에 해당하지 않는다”고 해명했다. 하지만 국방부는 지난해 전작권 전환 평가 결과에 대한 국회의 자료 제출 요구에 “(전작권과 관련한) 평가 항목 및 결과 등 구체적인 사항은 한·미 연합의 비밀로 답변이 제한된다”며 비공개 입장을 고수한 바 있다. 전작권 평가 추정이 가능한 자료를 공개하지 않겠다는 뜻이다. 군 당국이 지금까지 7년 넘게 국회에 관련 내용을 설명하지 않은 것은 사안의 민감성을 방증한다. 미국은 지난 3월 정동영 통일부 장관의 북한 구성시 우라늄 농축시설 존재 언급을 이유로 아직까지 북핵 동향 관련 정보 제공을 중단하고 있다. 한·미 동맹에 균열 징후가 이어지고 있는 만큼 책임 있는 당국자라면 단어 하나에도 신중을 기해 발언하는 것이 맞다. 한·미는 어제부터 1박2일 동안 한국군의 핵추진잠수함 보유와 우라늄 농축 등과 관련한 협의를 시작했다. 현 정부가 역점을 두고 있는 전작권 환수나 핵추진잠수함 보유 등은 결국 한·미 동맹의 튼튼한 신뢰가 기반이 돼야 순조롭게 진행될 수 있다는 점을 분명히 인식할 필요가 있다.
editorial.pullQuote: 책임 있는 당국자라면 단어 하나에도 신중을 기해 발언하는 것이 맞다.
summary: 국방장관이 전작권 전환 평가 수준 94%를 공개해 비밀 유출 논란 초래했다.
군 당국은 한·미 연합 비밀이라며 국회에 자료 제출을 거부해왔다.
한·미 동맹 신뢰가 기반되어야 전작권 환수 등이 순조롭게 진행된다.
editorial.stance: 정부는 민감한 안보 사안에서 일관성 있고 신중한 정보 관리로 한·미 신뢰를 지켜야 한다.
editorial.titleTr: Defense Minister's Remarks Invite Classified Information Leak Controversy
summaryTr: The defense minister disclosed a 94% readiness assessment level for the transition of wartime operational control, triggering controversy over secret information leaks.
Military authorities have refused to submit documents to the National Assembly, citing the information as a South Korea-U.S. alliance secret.
The smooth progress of regaining wartime operational control depends on building trust in the South Korea-U.S. alliance.
editorial.bodyTr:
Defense Minister An Kyou-paik has become embroiled in a controversy over leaked classified information after publicly disclosing the assessment level between South Korea and the United States regarding the transfer of operational control. During a business trip to Singapore last month, Minister An stated, "South Korea and the United States agreed that 94 percent of the conditions for the transfer of wartime operational control (OPCON) had already been met as of 2020, and we have sufficiently explained our capabilities." He reportedly met with U.S. lawmakers attending the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore to brief them on the South Korean military's ability to exercise operational control and to request cooperation.

Seoul and Washington have agreed to transfer operational control to the South Korean military once conditions are met, and are currently conducting assessments based on detailed manuals. However, the specific evaluation criteria and implementation levels are classified as a South Korea-U.S. joint military secret due to concerns that operational capability levels and defense postures could be exposed to North Korea and other adversaries.

Yet the head of the Defense Ministry publicly mentioned the specific figure of 94 percent, which military authorities on both sides have been reluctant to disclose. When controversy ensued, the Defense Ministry explained that it had presented only a rough figure to guarantee public awareness and understanding regarding the early recovery of operational control, and that it did not mention specific details and therefore did not constitute a breach of South Korea-U.S. joint military secrets. However, the ministry previously maintained a position of non-disclosure when rejecting the National Assembly's request for materials regarding last year's OPCON transfer assessment results, stating that responses were restricted as evaluation criteria and results constituted South Korea-U.S. joint military secrets. This amounts to refusing to disclose information from which OPCON assessments could be inferred.

The military authorities' refusal to explain related matters to the National Assembly for over seven years demonstrates the sensitivity of the issue. The United States has suspended the provision of information related to North Korean nuclear developments since March, citing Unification Minister Chung Dong-young's remarks about the existence of uranium enrichment facilities in a unified Korean peninsula. Given the continuing signs of fissures in the South Korea-U.S. alliance, responsible government officials must exercise caution in every word they speak. Starting yesterday, South Korea and the United States began one-and-a-half-day consultations regarding the South Korean military's acquisition of nuclear-powered submarines and uranium enrichment. It must be clearly recognized that priority initiatives of the current government—such as the recovery of operational control and the acquisition of nuclear-powered submarines—can only proceed smoothly when built on a strong foundation of trust in the South Korea-U.S. alliance.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: 국방장관의 비밀 유출을 비판하며 한·미 동맹의 신뢰와 안보 체계 유지를 강조하는 관료적·보수적 입장으로, 정부 신뢰성과 한·미 연합 기밀 유지를 핵심 프레임으로 삼음

editorial.title: Leaving for learning
editorial.body: Korea’s public education system pushes ambitious students out of school In most countries, dropping out of high school is treated as a warning sign. In South Korea, it is increasingly becoming an admissions strategy. A growing number of students are leaving school not because they have given up on education, but because they want more of it. They are calculating that the most efficient route to a university place lies outside the classroom. That paradox should concern policymakers far more than the headline numbers themselves. When ambitious students conclude that school attendance weakens their prospects, something in the system has gone badly awry. More than 10,000 first-year high school students nationwide discontinued their studies last year, reaching 10,450, a 6.1 percent increase from the previous year. At the same time, the number of high school equivalency exam graduates registering for the College Scholastic Ability Test climbed to 22,355 for the 2026 admissions cycle, the highest level in 31 years and nearly double the figure recorded in 2020. The increase is concentrated in some of the country’s most competitive school districts, including Seoul’s Gangnam area and non-standardized high schools in Gyeonggi Province. These are not students fleeing academic pressure. They are responding to it. For many families, leaving school and focusing exclusively on the national college entrance examination now appears less risky than remaining within the school grading system. Last year, the country introduced a five-tier grading system, replacing the traditional nine-tier structure. The change has helped accelerate the exodus from classrooms. The reform was intended to ease competition by expanding the proportion of students eligible for the top grade. Yet many families now believe that anything short of the highest tier significantly diminishes the chances of admission to elite universities. In effect, a broader top grade has not eased pressure so much as raised the perceived cost of falling below it. A reform designed to reduce anxiety has instead increased it. The problem extends beyond grading. The high school credit system was introduced to encourage students to explore their interests and design more personalized academic pathways. In practice, many are choosing courses according to perceived grading advantages rather than curiosity or aptitude. An irony lies at the center of this system. Universities are expanding interdisciplinary programs and increasing major-agnostic admissions in response to an economy being reshaped by artificial intelligence. High schools, meanwhile, continue to push students toward early specialization and relentless credential management. The crisis is not limited to high-achieving students. Many students with migrant backgrounds are also leaving classrooms, though for different reasons. Language barriers, inadequate bilingual instruction and weak support systems often leave them struggling academically and socially. Though different in origin, both trends reveal the same weakness. Public education is failing both the students seeking greater academic opportunity and those requiring greater institutional support. One group exits because the education system is too rigid; the other leaves because it is too indifferent to their needs. The answer is not another adjustment to grading thresholds or admissions formulas. South Korea must reconsider the purpose of secondary education itself. Schools should cultivate broad competence, intellectual flexibility and social cohesion, not merely sort students for an admissions race. When leaving school becomes a rational choice for the ambitious and an invisible fate for the disadvantaged, the problem lies not with individual students but with the architecture of education. A healthy education system should not force students to choose between learning and staying in school. Ultimately, the test of a school system is not how efficiently it ranks students, but whether it can retain and develop talent across the full spectrum of society. summaryTr: 매년 1만 명 이상의 한국 고등학생들이 학교를 떠나 교실 밖에서 대입 준비를 하고 있다.
성적 평가 개혁은 압박감을 완화하려던 취지와 달리 경쟁을 심화시켜 야심 있는 학생들의 자퇴를 부추겼다.
공교육은 유연성을 원하는 우수 학생과 지원이 필요한 취약계층 학생 모두에게 외면받고 있다.
editorial.bodyTr:
한국의 공교육 시스템이 야심 찬 학생들을 학교 밖으로 몰아내고 있다

대부분의 국가에서 고등학교 중퇴는 경고 신호로 취급된다. 남한에서는 점점 더 입시 전략으로 변모하고 있다. 학교를 떠나는 학생들의 수가 늘어나고 있는데, 이들은 교육을 포기했기 때문이 아니라 더 많은 교육을 원하기 때문이다. 그들은 대학 입학의 가장 효율적인 경로가 교실 밖에 있다고 계산하고 있다. 이러한 역설은 단순한 통계 수치 자체보다 정책 입안자들을 훨씬 더 우려하게 해야 한다. 야심 찬 학생들이 학교 출석이 전망을 약화시킨다고 결론지을 때, 시스템에 뭔가 심각한 문제가 생긴 것이다.

지난해 전국 고등학교 1학년 학생 1만 450명이 학업을 중단했으며, 이는 전년도 대비 6.1% 증가한 수치다. 동시에 2026학년도 수능 응시자 중 고등학교 동등학력 인정 시험 합격자 수는 2만 2,355명에 달해 31년 만에 최고치를 기록했으며, 2020년 수치의 거의 2배에 달한다. 증가 추세는 서울 강남지역과 경기도의 비평준화 고등학교를 포함한 경쟁이 가장 심한 학군에 집중되어 있다. 이들은 학업 압박에서 벗어나려는 학생들이 아니다. 학업 압박에 대응하고 있는 것이다.

많은 가정에서는 이제 학교를 떠나 대입시험에만 집중하는 것이 학교 성적 시스템 내에 머무르는 것보다 위험도가 낮다고 판단하고 있다. 지난해 한국은 기존의 9단계 등급제를 대체하는 5단계 등급제를 도입했다. 이 변화는 교실 이탈 현상을 가속화했다. 이 개혁은 최상위 등급에 해당하는 학생의 비율을 확대해 경쟁을 완화하려는 의도였다. 그러나 많은 가정은 이제 최상위 등급 미만의 어떤 등급이라도 명문대 입학 가능성을 크게 줄인다고 믿고 있다. 사실상 더 넓어진 최상위 등급이 압박감을 완화하기보다는 그 아래로 떨어질 경우의 인지된 비용을 증가시킨 것이다. 불안감을 줄이려고 고안된 개혁이 오히려 불안감을 증가시킨 것이다.

문제는 등급제를 넘어선다. 고등학교 학점제는 학생들이 자신의 관심사를 탐구하고 더욱 개인화된 학업 경로를 설계하도록 장려하기 위해 도입되었다. 그러나 실제로는 많은 학생들이 호기심이나 적성보다는 인지된 등급 이점에 따라 과목을 선택하고 있다.

이 시스템의 중심부에 아이러니가 있다. 대학들은 인공지능으로 인해 재편되고 있는 경제에 대응하기 위해 학제 간 프로그램을 확대하고 전공 무관 입시를 늘리고 있다. 한편 고등학교는 계속해서 학생들을 조기 전공화와 끝없는 자격 관리로 몰아가고 있다.

이 위기는 성적이 우수한 학생들로만 국한되지 않는다. 이주 배경을 가진 많은 학생들도 학교를 떠나고 있으나, 그 이유는 다르다. 언어 장벽, 불충분한 이중언어 교육, 약한 지원 시스템은 종종 그들이 학업적으로, 사회적으로 어려움을 겪게 만든다. 출발점이 다르지만, 두 추세 모두 동일한 약점을 드러낸다. 공교육은 더 큰 학업 기회를 찾는 학생들도, 더 큰 제도적 지원이 필요한 학생들도 모두 실패하고 있다. 한 집단은 교육 시스템이 너무 경직되어 있어서 떠나가고, 다른 집단은 그들의 필요에 너무 무관심해서 떠나간다.

답은 등급 기준이나 입시 공식의 또 다른 조정이 아니다. 한국은 중등교육 자체의 목적을 다시 생각해야 한다. 학교는 단순히 학생들을 입시 경쟁을 위해 분류하는 것이 아니라 폭넓은 역량, 지적 유연성, 사회적 응집력을 배양해야 한다. 학교를 떠나는 것이 야심 찬 학생들에게는 합리적 선택이 되고 소외된 학생들에게는 보이지 않는 숙명이 될 때, 문제는 개별 학생들에게 있는 것이 아니라 교육의 구조에 있다. 건강한 교육 시스템은 학생들이 학습과 학교 출석 사이에서 선택하도록 강요해서는 안 된다. 궁극적으로 학교 시스템의 진정한 시험은 얼마나 효율적으로 학생들을 순위 매기는가가 아니라 사회 전 계층의 인재를 유지하고 발전시킬 수 있는가이다.

leanScore.rationale: Frames educational inequality as a systemic/structural problem requiring institutional reform, criticizes competitive credential-driven system as harmful, and advocates for broader public education goals (social cohesion, flexibility) over market-like sorting mechanisms.

editorials.title: Leaving for learning
editorials.body: Korea’s public education system pushes ambitious students out of school In most countries, dropping out of high school is treated as a warning sign. In South Korea, it is increasingly becoming an admissions strategy. A growing number of students are leaving school not because they have given up on education, but because they want more of it. They are calculating that the most efficient route to a university place lies outside the classroom. That paradox should concern policymakers far more than the headline numbers themselves. When ambitious students conclude that school attendance weakens their prospects, something in the system has gone badly awry. More than 10,000 first-year high school students nationwide discontinued their studies last year, reaching 10,450, a 6.1 percent increase from the previous year. At the same time, the number of high school equivalency exam graduates registering for the College Scholastic Ability Test climbed to 22,355 for the 2026 admissions cycle, the highest level in 31 years and nearly double the figure recorded in 2020. The increase is concentrated in some of the country’s most competitive school districts, including Seoul’s Gangnam area and non-standardized high schools in Gyeonggi Province. These are not students fleeing academic pressure. They are responding to it. For many families, leaving school and focusing exclusively on the national college entrance examination now appears less risky than remaining within the school grading system. Last year, the country introduced a five-tier grading system, replacing the traditional nine-tier structure. The change has helped accelerate the exodus from classrooms. The reform was intended to ease competition by expanding the proportion of students eligible for the top grade. Yet many families now believe that anything short of the highest tier significantly diminishes the chances of admission to elite universities. In effect, a broader top grade has not eased pressure so much as raised the perceived cost of falling below it. A reform designed to reduce anxiety has instead increased it. The problem extends beyond grading. The high school credit system was introduced to encourage students to explore their interests and design more personalized academic pathways. In practice, many are choosing courses according to perceived grading advantages rather than curiosity or aptitude. An irony lies at the center of this system. Universities are expanding interdisciplinary programs and increasing major-agnostic admissions in response to an economy being reshaped by artificial intelligence. High schools, meanwhile, continue to push students toward early specialization and relentless credential management. The crisis is not limited to high-achieving students. Many students with migrant backgrounds are also leaving classrooms, though for different reasons. Language barriers, inadequate bilingual instruction and weak support systems often leave them struggling academically and socially. Though different in origin, both trends reveal the same weakness. Public education is failing both the students seeking greater academic opportunity and those requiring greater institutional support. One group exits because the education system is too rigid; the other leaves because it is too indifferent to their needs. The answer is not another adjustment to grading thresholds or admissions formulas. South Korea must reconsider the purpose of secondary education itself. Schools should cultivate broad competence, intellectual flexibility and social cohesion, not merely sort students for an admissions race. When leaving school becomes a rational choice for the ambitious and an invisible fate for the disadvantaged, the problem lies not with individual students but with the architecture of education. A healthy education system should not force students to choose between learning and staying in school. Ultimately, the test of a school system is not how efficiently it ranks students, but whether it can retain and develop talent across the full spectrum of society. summary: Over 10,000 South Korean high schoolers quit annually, seeking college prep outside classrooms.
New grading reforms meant to ease pressure instead raised stakes, pushing ambitious students to leave.
Public education fails both high-achievers seeking flexibility and disadvantaged students needing support.

summaryTr: 매년 1만 명 이상의 한국 고등학생들이 학교를 떠나 교실 밖에서 대입 준비를 하고 있다.
성적 평가 개혁은 압박감을 완화하려던 취지와 달리 경쟁을 심화시켜 야심 있는 학생들의 자퇴를 부추겼다.
공교육은 유연성을 원하는 우수 학생과 지원이 필요한 취약계층 학생 모두에게 외면받고 있다.

editorial.title: [사설] 내란 이어 외환 획책까지, 윤석열 징역 30년도 가볍다
editorial.body: 헌정사상 처음으로 전직 대통령이 대한민국의 군사상 이익을 해한 ‘일반이적’ 혐의로 유죄를 선고받았다. ‘내란 우두머리’ 혐의로 이미 무기징역을 선고받은 윤석열 전 대통령이 그 내란의 명분을 만들기 위해 평양에 무인기 침투를 지시한 혐의로 징역 30년을 선고받은 것이다.
editorial.pullQuote: 내란의 명분을 만들기 위해 평양에 무인기 침투를 지시한 혐의로 징역 30년을 선고받은 것이다.
summary: 전직 대통령이 '일반이적' 혐의로 유죄 선고받다.
내란 명분 조성 위해 평양 무인기 침투 지시.
징역 30년 선고, 무기징역에 이어 추가 처벌.
editorial.stance: 전직 대통령의 중대 국가반란 행위에 대한 엄중한 사법 판단을 보도함
editorial.titleTr: [Editorial] Sedition Followed by Foreign Exchange Schemes—30 Years Hard Labor for Yoon Suk-yeol Is Too Light
summaryTr: Former president convicted on charges of treason against the state.
Ordered infiltration of unmanned drones from Pyongyang to create pretext for insurrection.
Sentenced to 30 years imprisonment, additional punishment following life sentence.
editorial.bodyTr: For the first time in constitutional history, a former president has been convicted of treason that harmed South Korea's military interests. Former President Yoon Suk Yeol, who has already been sentenced to life imprisonment on charges of leading an insurrection, has now been sentenced to 30 years in prison on charges of ordering unmanned drone incursions into Pyongyang to create justification for that insurrection.

editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Sedition Followed by Foreign Exchange Schemes—30 Years Hard Labor for Yoon Suk-yeol Is Too Light
summaryTr: Former president convicted on charges of treason against the state.
Ordered infiltration of unmanned drones from Pyongyang to create pretext for insurrection.
Sentenced to 30 years imprisonment, additional punishment following life sentence.
editorials.bodyTr: For the first time in constitutional history, a former president has been convicted of treason that harmed South Korea's military interests. Former President Yoon Suk Yeol, who has already been sentenced to life imprisonment on charges of leading an insurrection, has now been sentenced to 30 years in prison on charges of ordering unmanned drone incursions into Pyongyang to create justification for that insurrection.

summaryTr: The deputy prime minister declared that youth employment will be the top policy priority.
Youth employment plummeted by 255,000 in May, with employment rates deteriorating severely.
Economic stimulus alone cannot solve the problem; structural reform of the labor market is essential.
editorial.bodyTr: Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Strategy and Finance Koo Yoon-chul said on the 12th, "I will make improving youth employment my top policy priority." With youth employment rates plummeting, including a sharp drop of 255,000 in the number of employed youth aged 15-29 in May, his statement appears to be a pledge to take some action. However, the youth employment problem is not something that can be solved through economic stimulus or enhanced incentives. editorials.titleTr: Paper Shortage, Ballot Counting Errors… Why Calls to Disband Election Commission Are Growing
summaryTr: Following a ballot shortage, counting errors have been detected in the Gyeonggi and North Jeolla superintendent elections.
Candidate vote tallies were recorded incorrectly, with results omitted and duplicated, threatening election credibility.
Repeated mishaps by the election commission have even raised calls for its dissolution.
editorials.bodyTr: Following the ballot shortage crisis, it has come to light that in the elections for the education superintendents of Gyeonggi Province and North Jeolla Province, vote tallies for candidates were recorded incorrectly, with vote counting results either omitted or entered in duplicate. In terms of the severity of the matter, this is more serious than the ballot shortage. While the ballot shortage was a result of incompetence, the vote counting errors threaten the very credibility of the election itself. The election commission has caused problems multiple times before, but never to such a chaotic degree.
